sections.title: Credits
sections.notes: Standard publication credits.

sections.title: Contents
sections.notes: Table of contents.

sections.title: Framing Fiction – Food Chain
sections.notes: Opening fiction establishing the mood and themes of the Nosferatu curse, transformation, and survival.

sections.title: A Hosting of Historians
sections.notes: Introduction to the historical section, explaining how Nosferatu pass down their history through oral tradition.

sections.title: The Earliest Nights
sections.notes: The legendary origin story of Nosferatu, from the hunter who was Embraced by Zillah.

sections.title: The Downward Spiral
sections.notes: Nosferatu's transformation and his growing hatred of monsters, including himself.

sections.title: Bound in Blood
sections.notes: The story of blood bonds, Nosferatu's childer, and the woman who escaped his control.

sections.title: The Curse
sections.notes: Caine's curse upon Nosferatu and all his descendants, creating the hideous appearance that defines the clan.

sections.title: The Second Story: A Scientific History
sections.anchor: ## the  seCOnd  stOry: a sCientiFiC  histOry
sections.notes: An alternative, scientific interpretation of Nosferatu origins, rejecting the Caine legend.

sections.title: The Nictuku Enigma
sections.notes: Discussion of the legendary Nictuku, Nosferatu's eldest childer who hunt the clan, and the mystery of the Matriarch.

sections.title: The Third Tale: A Conspiracy Theory
sections.anchor: ## the  third  tale: a COnspiraCy  theOry
sections.notes: A theory that Nosferatu have been building underground civilizations since the beginning of human cities.

sections.title: The Tigris, the Euphrates, and the Nile
sections.anchor: ## the  tigris,  the  euphrates  and  the  nile
sections.notes: Early Nosferatu civilizations in Mesopotamia and Egypt, living in irrigation canals and beneath rivers.

sections.title: The Roman Empire
sections.notes: Nosferatu influence on Roman sewer systems, particularly the Cloaca Maxima, and the development of underground domains.

sections.title: The European Middle Ages
sections.notes: Nosferatu adaptation to medieval cities, use of waste management as a weapon, and survival during the Dark Ages.

sections.title: The Importance of Waste
sections.notes: Explanation of how Nosferatu maintain city infrastructure and why waste management is essential to their survival.

sections.title: Stagnating in the Renaissance
sections.notes: Nosferatu during the Renaissance and the birth of the Camarilla, with differences between Sewer Rats and Creeps.

sections.title: The Embrace
sections.notes: How Nosferatu choose their childer and the transformation that occurs during the Embrace.

sections.title: The Thousand Forms of Flesh
sections.notes: Description of various Nosferatu archetypes and the different forms their curse can take.

sections.title: Nosferatu Culture
sections.notes: Overview of Nosferatu social structures, from solitaries to broods to hostings.

sections.title: Sects and Politics
sections.notes: Nosferatu relationships with Camarilla, Sabbat, Anarchs, and Autarkis, and their political positions.

sections.title: Kingdoms of the Underworld
sections.notes: Detailed description of Nosferatu underground domains, their structure, culture, and activities.

sections.title: Relations with Other Clans and Supernaturals
sections.notes: Nosferatu relationships with other Kindred clans, sects, and supernatural beings.

sections.title: A Festering Heap of Game Mechanics
sections.anchor: ## a Festering  heap OF  game  meChaniCs
sections.notes: Introduction to the mechanics section.

sections.title: New Background: Information Network
sections.notes: Rules for the Information Network background, representing Nosferatu connections to SchreckNET and other information sources.

sections.title: Nosferatu and the Lore Ability
sections.notes: How Nosferatu use the Lore ability and their role as keepers of Kindred history and secrets.

sections.title: Merits and Flaws
sections.notes: Nosferatu-specific Merits and Flaws that reflect their curse and adaptations.

sections.title: Clan Disciplines
children: ## sOng in the darK Animalism Level Six
sections.notes: Detailed discussion of how Nosferatu use their clan Disciplines (Animalism, Obfuscate, Potence) in creative and practical ways.

sections.title: The Nosferatu's Chosen
sections.notes: Character archetypes and concepts for Nosferatu players, with detailed descriptions of each archetype's background, concept, and roleplaying hints.

sections.title: Venerable Nosferatu
sections.notes: Profiles of notable Nosferatu elders and their roles in Kindred history and current events.
